name: silent-spring
description: Rachel Carson's Silent Spring — environmental science and activism toolkit exposing how DDT and synthetic pesticides poison ecosystems, accumulate in food chains, and threaten all life, launching the modern environmental movement
useCase: Understand the pesticide crisis, learn ecological principles, identify alternatives to chemical control, and take action for environmental health
knownRisks: Some scientific details have been updated since 1962 — but Carson's core findings remain valid
The book has been criticized for creating 'chemophobia' and leading to overly restrictive pesticide policies
DDT remains controversial for malaria control in some developing countries
